zh.lifeSummary: Graf Of Wasserburg ENGELBERT (Graf of Wasserburg) von DIESSEN** (1099-1161) Von Diessen於1099年出生。他至少有1個兒子。

nameMeanings.name: Von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Diessen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch, Flemish, and North German: patronymic, formed with sen ‘son’, from the personal name Dries .

variants.name: Driesen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Hans.
Dutch, Flemish, and North German: variant of   Driessen  .

variants.name: Dries
meanings: Dutch and German: from the personal name Dries, a short form of Andries .
Dutch: variant of Van den Dries, a topographic name from dries ‘common meadow, not of good quality’.

nameMeanings.name: ENGELBERT
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from engel (representing Ingal, from Ing, the name of a Germanic god), Angel ‘Angle’, or engel ‘angel’ + berht ‘bright’.

variants.name: Engelbrecht
meanings: from engel (representing Ingal, from Ing, the name of a Germanic god), Angel ‘Angle’, or engel ‘angel’ + berht ‘bright’.

nameMeanings.name: (Graf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: See  Grey .

variants.name: Grey
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin a nickname for someone with grey hair or a grey beard (from Old English græg). In Scotland and Ireland it has also been used as a translation of various Gaelic surnames derived from riabhach ‘brindled, grey’, such as Mac Riabhaich.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Ivo  , derived from the oblique case of the name.

nameMeanings.name: DIESSEN**
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch, Flemish, and North German: patronymic, formed with sen ‘son’, from the personal name Dries .

variants.name: Driesen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Hans.
Dutch, Flemish, and North German: variant of   Driessen  .

variants.name: Dries
meanings: Dutch and German: from the personal name Dries, a short form of Andries .
Dutch: variant of Van den Dries, a topographic name from dries ‘common meadow, not of good quality’.
